textByHash.936f3b7875773e9a7166309b993351be66de0580: Yes. We're guarding this entrance against demonic excursions.
textByHash.ad6b09dea0461e18f672d17ec94540ec14132226: Not super well, I'm afraid. It's a nerve wracking post, and it's taking its toll on my soldiers.
textByHash.debf2ff39b108f4416e3f383a2b6936539525f70: Not in any official capacity. Making any sort of move might spark a chain reaction.
textByHash.44b31dd44c02cbf68568e70809437742641c69b9: It would be good to know what's going on down there. If you can handle yourself, and you can keep an eye on the demons, you might be able to help.
textByHash.a463c1ec3792318f0390fe555ee030a9cb402b3d: We just need to know if it looks like they're preparing for anything. But if you do this, you're on your own. If anything happens, I won't be able to send help.
textByHash.c0252bbeff4f6ff279899b3fc5500b470c13b1c9: Some of them are afraid, others impatient. That's the biggest problem actually. They're all having different reactions. It's making it difficult to work as a unit.
textByHash.bdd7048c97da0114c48d1fecd67b2028687334e5: No, they're good soldiers. I suspect it has something to do with those cultists and their little chats.
textByHash.a7c4def37b5db2f3e150aae8006bf28fee3170fd: Not that we can do much about that. We can't afford to initiate hostilities. There's a lot at stake, and I'm honestly not sure we could handle a direct confrontation.
